content.text: When you give an LLM a tool, you describe it using a

— a vocabulary for describing the shape of a JSON value. The schema tells the LLM: what arguments does this tool accept, what type is each argument, which are required, and what values are valid. The LLM uses this description to generate a correctly-typed JSON object when it decides to call the tool.
The schema format was standardised by OpenAI's function-calling API and is now used across all major providers (Anthropic, Google, Cohere, Mistral). At the top level, each tool has a

The LLM reads name, description, and each property's description to decide WHEN to call the tool and WHAT arguments to pass. Clear, specific descriptions are the single biggest factor in tool reliability.
Every property in the parameters object has a
. The types map directly to JSON/Python types:
Rarely used alone; combined with anyOf
Most tools have flat (non-nested) arguments — a collection of scalar properties. Always include
and list every required field explicitly. This prevents the LLM from hallucinating extra fields.

# Calling the tool from Python (after LLM generates the call):
def search_documents(
    query: str,
    top_k: int = 5,
    department: str = "all",
    include_metadata: bool = False,
) -> list[dict]:
    ...  # your implementation
When a tool argument is itself a structured object, nest a
block inside the argument's type definition. Add
sub-schema that describes the elements. Arrays of scalars are straightforward; arrays of objects require a nested schema in
are optional — the LLM may omit them. If a field can be either a value or
— some providers do not support the array-of-types shorthand.
Instead of writing JSON Schemas by hand, define tool arguments as Pydantic models and call
. Both LangChain and the OpenAI SDK accept Pydantic models directly and generate the schema automatically.

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import Optional
import json

class SearchArgs(BaseModel):
    query: str = Field(description="Natural language search query.")
    top_k: int = Field(default=5, ge=1, le=20, description="Number of results.")
    department: str = Field(
        default="all",
        pattern="^(finance|hr|legal|engineering|all)$",
        description="Department filter.",
    )
    include_metadata: bool = Field(default=False)

# Generate the schema
schema = SearchArgs.model_json_schema()
print(json.dumps(schema, indent=2))

# Use with OpenAI SDK directly
from openai import OpenAI
client = OpenAI()

response = client.chat.completions.create(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Search for Q3 earnings in finance."}],
    tools=[{
        "type": "function",
        "function": {
            "name": "search_documents",
            "description": "Search internal documents.",
            "parameters": schema,
        },
    }],
    tool_choice="auto",
)
tool_call = response.choices[0].message.tool_calls[0]
args = SearchArgs.model_validate_json(tool_call.function.arguments)
print(args.query, args.top_k, args.department)
The LLM can generate arguments that fail schema constraints — rare but possible, especially with complex nested schemas or weak models. Validate before executing the tool.
say what the tool does and when to use it. Include examples of values if the domain is specialised.
every property needs one. Include the expected format for strings (ISO 8601, currency code, etc.).
list every field the tool cannot function without. Do not mark optional parameters as required.
always include at every level. Prevents the LLM from hallucinating extra keys.
if a string can only take N known values, list them. The LLM respects enum constraints reliably.
flatten where possible. Nested schemas increase error rates. Two levels deep is usually the limit.
Most tool-calling failures are schema description failures, not model failures.

If a tool is called with wrong arguments, improve the description before blaming the model. Add examples to the description, tighten enum constraints, and check that required fields are actually marked required.
